logo_url.markdownDescription: Absolute URL or absolute path to the site logo that will be displayed on the entrance page and the browser’s tab (favicon). A square image works best. Default: Sveltia logo.

show_preview_links.markdownDescription: Whether to show site preview links. Default: `true`.

singletons.description: Set of singleton files, such as the site configuration file or the homepage file. They are not part of any collection and can be accessed directly through the collection list. singletons.markdownDescription: Set of singleton files, such as the site configuration file or the homepage file. i18n.description: Global i18n options.
i18n.markdownDescription: Global i18n options.

output.markdownDescription: Data output options. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#controlling-data-output) for details.

name.description: Backend name.
name.markdownDescription: Backend name.

repo.description: Repository identifier. Required for Git backends. GitHub/Gitea/Forgejo: organization/user name and repository name joined by a slash, e.g. `owner/repo`. GitLab: namespace and project name joined by a slash, e.g. `group/project` or `group/subgroup/project`.
repo.markdownDescription: Repository identifier. Required for Git backends. GitHub/Gitea/Forgejo: organization/user name and repository name joined by a slash, e.g. `owner/repo`. GitLab: namespace and project name joined by a slash, e.g. `group/project` or `group/subgroup/project`.

branch.description: Git branch name. If omitted, the default branch, usually `main` or `master`, will be used. Git backends only.
branch.markdownDescription: Git branch name. If omitted, the default branch, usually `main` or `master`, will be used. Git backends only.

api_root.description: REST API endpoint for the backend. Git backends only. Required when using GitHub Enterprise Server, a self-hosted GitLab/Gitea/Forgejo instance. Default: `https://api.github.com` (GitHub), `https://gitlab.com/api/v4` (GitLab) or `https://gitea.com/api/v1` (Gitea/Forgejo).
api_root.markdownDescription: REST API endpoint for the backend. Git backends only. Required when using GitHub Enterprise Server, a self-hosted GitLab/Gitea/Forgejo instance. Default: `https://api.github.com` (GitHub), `https://gitlab.com/api/v4` (GitLab) or `https://gitea.com/api/v1` (Gitea/Forgejo).

graphql_api_root.description: GraphQL API endpoint for the backend. Git backends only. Default: inferred from `api_root` option value.
graphql_api_root.markdownDescription: GraphQL API endpoint for the backend. Git backends only. Default: inferred from `api_root` option value.

site_domain.description: Site domain used for OAuth, which will be included in the `site_id` param to be sent to the API endpoint. Git backends only. Default: [`location.hostname`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Location/hostname).
site_domain.markdownDescription: Site domain used for OAuth, which will be included in the `site_id` param to be sent to the API endpoint. Git backends only. Default: [`location.hostname`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Location/hostname).

base_url.description: OAuth base URL origin. Git backends only. Required when using an OAuth client other than Netlify, including [Sveltia CMS Authenticator](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms-auth). Default: `https://api.netlify.com` (GitHub), `https://gitlab.com` (GitLab) or `https://gitea.com/` (Gitea/Forgejo).
base_url.markdownDescription: OAuth base URL origin. Git backends only. Required when using an OAuth client other than Netlify, including [Sveltia CMS Authenticator](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms-auth). Default: `https://api.netlify.com` (GitHub), `https://gitlab.com` (GitLab) or `https://gitea.com/` (Gitea/Forgejo).

auth_endpoint.description: OAuth base URL path. Git backends only. Default: `auth` (GitHub) or `oauth/authorize` (GitLab).
auth_endpoint.markdownDescription: OAuth base URL path. Git backends only. Default: `auth` (GitHub) or `oauth/authorize` (GitLab).

auth_type.description: OAuth authentication method. GitLab only. Default: `pkce`.
auth_type.markdownDescription: OAuth authentication method. GitLab only. Default: `pkce`.

app_id.description: OAuth application ID. GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo only. Required for Gitea/Forgejo.
app_id.markdownDescription: OAuth application ID. GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo only. Required for Gitea/Forgejo.

commit_messages.description: Custom commit messages. Git backends only.
commit_messages.markdownDescription: Custom commit messages. Git backends only.

preview_context.description: Deploy preview link context. GitHub only.
preview_context.markdownDescription: Deploy preview link context. GitHub only.

cms_label_prefix.description: Pull request label prefix for Editorial Workflow. Git backends only. Default: `sveltia-cms/`.
cms_label_prefix.markdownDescription: Pull request label prefix for Editorial Workflow. Git backends only. Default: `sveltia-cms/`.

squash_merges.description: Whether to use squash marge for Editorial Workflow. Git backends only. Default: `false`.
squash_merges.markdownDescription: Whether to use squash marge for Editorial Workflow. Git backends only. Default: `false`.

open_authoring.description: Whether to use Open Authoring. Git backends only. Default: `false`.

automatic_deployments.deprecationMessage: Use the new `skip_ci` option instead, which is more intuitive. `automatic_deployments: false` is equivalent to `skip_ci: true`, and `automatic_deployments: true` is equivalent to `skip_ci: false`. See our README https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#disabling-automatic-deployments for details.

skip_ci.description: Whether to enable or disable automatic deployments with any connected CI/CD provider, such as GitHub Actions or Cloudflare Pages. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#disabling-automatic-deployments) for details.
skip_ci.markdownDescription: Whether to enable or disable automatic deployments with any connected CI/CD provider, such as GitHub Actions or Cloudflare Pages. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#disabling-automatic-deployments) for details.

Default: `false`, meaning that the original file name is kept by default, while Netlify/Decap CMS forces to slugify file names. If set to `true`, for example, `Hello World (1).webp` would be `hello-world-1.webp`.

transformations.description: File transformation option map. The key is an original format like `png` or `jpeg`. It can also be `raster_image` that matches any supported raster image format. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#optimizing-images-for-upload) for details.
transformations.markdownDescription: File transformation option map. The key is an original format like `png` or `jpeg`. It can also be `raster_image` that matches any supported raster image format. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#optimizing-images-for-upload) for details.

name.markdownDescription: Unique identifier for the collection.

label.description: Label of the field to be displayed in the editor UI. Default: `name` option value.
label.markdownDescription: Label of the field to be displayed in the editor UI. Default: `name` option value.

label_singular.description: Singular UI label. It will be Blog Post if the `label` is Blog Posts, for example. Default: `label` option value.

icon.markdownDescription: Name of a [Material Symbols icon](https://fonts.google.com/icons?icon.set=Material+Symbols) to be displayed in the collection list.

identifier_field.description: Field name to be used as the title and slug of an entry. Entry collection only. Default: `title`.
identifier_field.markdownDescription: Field name to be used as the title and slug of an entry. Entry collection only. Default: `title`.

files.description: A set of files. File collection only.
files.markdownDescription: A set of files. File collection only.

folder.markdownDescription: Base folder path relative to the project root. Entry collection only.

fields.markdownDescription: Set of fields to be included in entries. Entry collection only.

path.markdownDescription: File path relative to `folder`, without a file extension. Entry collection only.

media_folder.description: Internal media folder path for the collection. This overrides the global `media_folder` option. It can be a relative path from the project root if it starts with a slash. Otherwise it’s a path relative to the entry. If this option is omitted, the global `media_folder` option value is used. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#using-a-custom-media-folder-for-a-collection) for details.
media_folder.markdownDescription: Internal media folder path for the collection. This overrides the global `media_folder` option. It can be a relative path from the project root if it starts with a slash. Otherwise it’s a path relative to the entry. If this option is omitted, the global `media_folder` option value is used. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#using-a-custom-media-folder-for-a-collection) for details.

public_folder.description: Public media folder path for an entry collection. This overrides the global `public_folder` option. Default: `media_folder` option value.
public_folder.markdownDescription: Public media folder path for an entry collection. This overrides the global `public_folder` option. Default: `media_folder` option value.

hide.description: Whether to hide the collection in the UI. Default: `false`.
hide.markdownDescription: Whether to hide the collection in the UI. Default: `false`.

create.description: Whether to allow users to create entries in the collection. Entry collection only. Default: `false`.
create.markdownDescription: Whether to allow users to create entries in the collection. Entry collection only. Default: `false`.

delete.description: Whether to allow users to delete entries in the collection. Entry collection only. Default: `true`.
delete.markdownDescription: Whether to allow users to delete entries in the collection. Entry collection only. Default: `true`.

publish.description: Whether to show the publishing control UI for Editorial Workflow. Default: `true`.
publish.markdownDescription: Whether to show the publishing control UI for Editorial Workflow. Default: `true`.

extension.description: File extension. Entry collection only. Default: `md`.
extension.markdownDescription: File extension. Entry collection only. Default: `md`.

format.description: File format. It should match the file extension. Default: `yaml-frontmatter`.
format.markdownDescription: File format. It should match the file extension. Default: `yaml-frontmatter`.

frontmatter_delimiter.description: Delimiters to be used for the front matter format. Default: depends on the front matter type.
frontmatter_delimiter.markdownDescription: Delimiters to be used for the front matter format. Default: depends on the front matter type.

slug.description: Item slug template. Entry collection only. Default: `identifier_field` option value. It’s possible to [localize the slug](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#localizing-entry-slugs) or [use a random ID](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#using-a-random-id-for-an-entry-slug). Also, it’s possible to show a special slug editor field in initial entry drafts by using `{{fields._slug}}` (with an underscore prefix) or `{{fields._slug | localize}}` (to localize the slug).
slug.markdownDescription: Item slug template. Entry collection only. Default: `identifier_field` option value. It’s possible to [localize the slug](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#localizing-entry-slugs) or [use a random ID](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#using-a-random-id-for-an-entry-slug). Also, it’s possible to show a special slug editor field in initial entry drafts by using `{{fields._slug}}` (with an underscore prefix) or `{{fields._slug | localize}}` (to localize the slug).

slug_length.description: The maximum number of characters allowed for an entry slug. Entry collection only. Default: `Infinity`.
slug_length.markdownDescription: The maximum number of characters allowed for an entry slug. Entry collection only. Default: `Infinity`.

summary.description: Entry summary template. Entry collection only. Default: `identifier_field`.
summary.markdownDescription: Entry summary template. Entry collection only. Default: `identifier_field`.

sortable_fields.description: Custom sortable fields. Entry collection only. Default: `title`, `name`, `date`, `author` and `description`. For a Git backend, commit author and commit date are also included by default. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#specifying-default-sort-field-and-direction) for details.
sortable_fields.markdownDescription: Custom sortable fields. Entry collection only. Default: `title`, `name`, `date`, `author` and `description`. For a Git backend, commit author and commit date are also included by default. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#specifying-default-sort-field-and-direction) for details.

view_filters.description: View filters to be used in the entry list. Entry collection only.
view_filters.markdownDescription: View filters to be used in the entry list. Entry collection only.

view_groups.description: View groups to be used in the entry list. Entry collection only.
view_groups.markdownDescription: View groups to be used in the entry list. Entry collection only.

i18n.description: I18n options. Default: `false`.
i18n.markdownDescription: I18n options. Default: `false`.

nested.description: Options for a nested collection. Entry collection only.
nested.markdownDescription: Options for a nested collection. Entry collection only.

meta.description: Meta data for a nested collection. Entry collection only.
meta.markdownDescription: Meta data for a nested collection. Entry collection only.

index_file.description: Index file inclusion options. Entry collection only. If `true`, the default index file name is `_index`, which is used for Hugo’s special index file. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#including-hugos-special-index-file-in-a-folder-collection) for details.
index_file.markdownDescription: Index file inclusion options. Entry collection only. If `true`, the default index file name is `_index`, which is used for Hugo’s special index file. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#including-hugos-special-index-file-in-a-folder-collection) for details.

yaml_quote.description: Whether to double-quote all the strings values if the YAML format is used for file output. Default: `false`.
yaml_quote.markdownDescription: Whether to double-quote all the strings values if the YAML format is used for file output. Default: `false`.
yaml_quote.deprecationMessage: Use the global YAML format options. `yaml_quote: true` is equivalent to `quote: double`. See our README https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#controlling-data-output for details.

thumbnail.description: A field key path to be used to find an entry thumbnail displayed on the entry list. A nested field can be specified using dot notation, e.g. `heroImage.src`. A wildcard in the key path is also supported, e.g. `images.*.src`. Multiple key paths can be specified as an array for fallbacks. If this option is omitted, the `name` of any non-nested, non-empty field using the Image or File widget is used. Entry collection only.
thumbnail.markdownDescription: A field key path to be used to find an entry thumbnail displayed on the entry list. A nested field can be specified using dot notation, e.g. `heroImage.src`. canonical_slug.description: Property name and value template used to add a canonical slug to entry files, which helps Sveltia CMS and some frameworks to link localized files when entry slugs are localized. The default property name is `translationKey` used in Hugo’s multilingual support, and the default value is the default locale’s slug. See our [README](https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms#localizing-entry-slugs) for details.

omit_default_locale_from_filename.description: Whether to exclude the default locale from entry filenames. Default: `false`. This option applies to entry collections with the `multiple_files` i18n structure enabled, as well as to file/singleton collection items with the `file` path ending with `.{{locale}}.<extension>`, aiming to support [Zola’s multilingual sites](https://www.getzola.org/documentation/content/multilingual/).
